Pelvic Health Physical Therapy · Cincinnati

Redefine Your Normal. Reclaim Your Well-Being.

Leaking, pelvic pain, pressure, pain with sex, the changes that come with pregnancy or menopause — these things are common, and so often brushed off. They don't have to be something you just live with. We help you feel heard, find the real cause, and get back to feeling like yourself.

Serving the Greater Cincinnati area One-on-one, whole-body care
Conditions we treat

When Something Feels Off, Answers Start Here

You don't need a diagnosis to come in. These are the things we help with every day.

Our philosophy

Everything is connected. It starts at the pelvis.

The pelvis and pelvic floor support your bladder, bowel, core, and so much of how your body moves through the day — and they rarely work alone. When one part is off, the rest of you feels it.

So we don't chase symptoms in isolation. We take the time to understand your history, find the root cause, and build a plan around your real goals — then stay with you through the seasons of your life.

How we help

Skilled, hands-on care — tailored to you.

Your plan blends the right tools for what's going on, always explained as we go and never rushed.

Pelvic Health Physical Therapy · Cincinnati

It's common. But it's not your normal.

If you've been told this is just part of having kids, getting older, or staying active — consider this your second opinion. We dig for the why behind your symptoms and build a plan to actually address it, not just quiet it down.

Book a consultation Read common questions
What we help with

Finding the root cause, not just managing symptoms.

Most people don't come in asking for a specific technique — they come in because something feels off. Here's where we can help, in plain terms.

The how: depending on what's going on, your plan might blend hands-on manual therapy, guided exercise and core retraining, dry needling, visceral mobilization, and functional movement and postural control. We'll always explain what we're doing and why — and never do anything you're not comfortable with.

Pillar Webinar Series

Pelvic health, explained.

Clear, science-backed education for patients, clinicians, and the community — inclusive, practical, and refreshingly free of jargon and shame. Whether you're navigating your own pelvic health or sharpening your clinical skills, there's a seat for you.

Featured session
June 15
2026
6:00–7:00 PM ET · Online
Sign up free
Can't make it live? Sign up and we'll send the recording.
60-minute webinar · Q&A included

A Little Sag, A Lot of Questions

Vaginal prolapse — the pelvic floor's fight against gravity.

A candid, judgment-free conversation about prolapse and pelvic floor support. We'll break down what prolapse actually is, why symptoms like pressure, bulging, heaviness, and that "tampon won't stay" feeling happen, and what you can do about it.

We'll cover prevention, treatment options, exercise myths, bladder and bowel impacts, and practical ways to support your pelvic floor day to day — without fear or shame.

Perfect for postpartum moms, active women, peri/menopausal women, or anyone wondering, "Wait — is this normal?"

Who these are for

  • Patients seeking trustworthy pelvic health education
  • Clinicians looking for advanced or niche topics
  • Parents & caregivers wanting age-appropriate guidance
  • Fitness pros who want pelvic-aware education

No prior pelvic health knowledge required, unless a session notes otherwise.

What you'll get

  • Clear explanations, no medical jargon
  • Practical takeaways you can use right away
  • Live Q&A during real-time sessions
  • Replay access when available

PT, DPT, PRPC · Founder

Dr. Michelle Koval

She / Her / Hers · Owner & Pelvic Floor Therapist

Michelle started Pillar for the people who've been told their symptoms are "just normal." Her whole approach is built on listening first, treating the whole body, and finding the real reason something feels off.

Book with MichelleLet's talk
About Michelle

Michelle founded Pillar because she was tired of watching people get brushed off. Too many patients came to her after being told their symptoms were just part of having kids, getting older, or being active — and too many had never been given a real answer. She built Pillar to be the opposite of that: a place where you're actually listened to, where nothing's too awkward to say out loud, and where the goal is figuring out the why, not just managing the what.

Her approach is whole-body and one-on-one. You'll have her full attention for a full hour, every visit — no getting passed around, no rushing. She looks at how everything connects: your core, your breath, your posture, your hips, and the pelvic floor at the center of it all.

Michelle works with people across every life stage — pregnancy and postpartum, prolapse and pressure, pelvic pain, painful sex, the changes that come with perimenopause, and bladder and bowel concerns. Let's talk
Men's pelvic health

Yes, men have a pelvic floor too.

Pelvic floor problems aren't just a women's health thing. Men have a pelvic floor, and it can cause just as much trouble when it isn't working right — leaking, pain, constipation, changes after surgery. The good news: it's treatable, and it's not something you just have to live with.

Book a consultationLet's talk
"You have a pelvic floor. When it's not working right, we can help — directly, and without the awkwardness."
What we help with

The stuff that rarely gets talked about — and almost never gets treated this directly.

After prostate surgery

Leaking, urgency, and loss of control are incredibly common after a prostatectomy — and very treatable. We help you rebuild strength and coordination so you can get back to normal life faster.

Male pelvic pain

Pain in the pelvis, groin, testicles, or tailbone often traces back to the pelvic floor. We work to calm overactive muscles and get to the root of what's driving it.

Erectile & sexual health

The pelvic floor plays a real role in sexual function. It rarely gets talked about — but it's a normal, treatable part of what we do.

Constipation & bowel changes

Straining, incomplete emptying, and bowel changes are often a coordination problem, not just a diet one. We help retrain how everything works together.

Bladder changes

Urgency, frequency, waking up at night, or leaking — we figure out why it's happening and retrain the system so you can trust your body again.

Before & after surgery

Whether you're preparing for a procedure or recovering from one, targeted pelvic floor work can make the whole process smoother.

Why this matters

Nobody talks about this — that's the problem.

Most men have never been told the pelvic floor exists, let alone that it could be behind what they're dealing with. So they push through, stay quiet, or chalk it up to aging. It isn't.

Pelvic floor physical therapy is direct, practical, and judgment-free — the same focused, one-on-one care we give everyone, aimed squarely at the things that usually go unaddressed.

What to expect

A full hour with the same therapist. No rushing, no getting passed around.

04

Built around your goals and how your body actually works.

Common questions

Good to know

Do I need a referral from my doctor?
Nope. Ohio allows direct access to physical therapy, so you can book straight in. If you'd like us to loop in your doctor or urologist, we're glad to be part of your care team.
What happens at the first visit?
We listen first — your history, your symptoms, and what you want to get back to — then do an assessment and build a plan together. You're in control the whole way, and we explain everything before we do it.
Is this actually common for men?
More than you'd think. Pelvic floor issues affect men of every age — after surgery, after injury, or seemingly out of nowhere. You're not an outlier, and you're not overreacting by getting it looked at.

Take the first step.

If any of this sounds familiar, let's talk. No pressure, no awkwardness — just a real conversation about what's going on and how we can help.
